mission.text: Ship reliable OpenClaw automations with human-in-the-loop clarity: fast iteration, traceable decisions, and calm operations.

agents.name: Claw Prime
agents.currentWork: Overnight autonomous task list — Mission Control fixes
agents.handoffRules: Escalate security or destructive ops; delegate codegen to Cursor CLI path.

agents.name: Scout
agents.handoffRules: Return citations + uncertainty; never exfiltrate private data.

agents.name: Forge
agents.handoffRules: Prefer small PR-sized diffs; match repo conventions.

agents.name: Relay
agents.handoffRules: Batch non-urgent checks; surface blockers within 2 minutes.

agents.name: Sentinel
agents.handoffRules: No commits/pushes without explicit human approval.

tasks.title: Wire React Context store to all pages
tasks.description: Hydrate from data/sample.json; expose CRUD for tasks and calendar events.

tasks.title: SSE real-time state sync
tasks.description: Add /api/state/stream endpoint using Server-Sent Events; wire EventSource into store.

tasks.title: Replace all mock data with real agent system data
tasks.description: Connect memories, docs, cron jobs, and agent status to actual OpenClaw workspace files.

tasks.title: Improve Office screen layout and animations
tasks.description: Grid layout, pulse animations, hover tooltips, meeting zone highlight, idle dimming.

tasks.title: General polish pass — fix layouts and empty states
tasks.description: Fix broken layouts, verify all 9 screens load without errors, improve empty states.

projects.name: Mission Control UI
projects.description: Unified OpenClaw dashboard: tasks, calendar, docs, team, office.
projects.suggestedNextAction: Complete mock data replacement, then Office screen improvements.

projects.name: Documentation & guides
projects.description: Workspace docs, policies, and onboarding content.

milestones.title: Cross-link MC tasks to policy docs

projects.suggestedNextAction: Keep AGENTS.md and SOUL.md discoverable from the dashboard.

memories.title: Workspace initialization & ForgeERP fixes
memories.content: First session: established Claw identity, SOUL.md, USER.md. Then completed 9 ForgeERP fixes: config drift, migrations, Swagger, dashboard bug, Jest tests, CI/CD, security/observability, docs, env fixes.

memories.title: Mission Control sync & model audit
memories.content: Discord session: checked Cursor SSE progress, audited agent models, started mock audit. Discovered mission-control-integration skill. Identified that activity feed and calendar need real data sources.

memories.title: Overnight autonomous task execution
memories.content: Mohit sent overnight task list via Telegram. Working through: critical fixes (no localhost fetch, fix read paths), SSE verification, agent model audit, mock audit, real data replacement, Office improvements, polish pass, morning report.

memories.title: Security boundary for MEMORY.md
memories.content: MEMORY.md is main-session only; never load in shared contexts. Treat as sensitive personal context. Aligned with AGENTS.md red lines.

docs.title: Mission Control — Frontend architecture
docs.body: App Router layouts wrap a client Provider. State lives in lib/store.tsx via React Context. SSE endpoint at /api/state/stream provides real-time updates via EventSource. data/state.json is the source of truth on disk.

docs.title: AGENTS.md — Workspace agent rules
docs.body: Session startup sequence, memory management, red lines (no git commit/push without approval), group chat behavior, heartbeat rules, model routing, Cursor CLI preferences.

docs.title: SOUL.md — Agent identity & persona
docs.body: Core truths: be genuinely helpful, have opinions, be resourceful before asking, earn trust through competence, remember you're a guest. Boundaries: private things stay private, ask before acting externally.

docs.title: TOOLS.md — Local setup notes
docs.body: Cursor Agent CLI at %LOCALAPPDATA%\cursor-agent. Gateway auth at 127.0.0.1:18789. Model stack: Step 3.5 Flash → DeepSeek V3 → Gemini Flash-Lite → Kilo auto.

docs.title: MEMORY.md — Long-term memory
docs.body: ForgeERP fixes completed 2026-03-26: config drift, migrations, Swagger, dashboard bug, tests, CI/CD, security, docs, env. Remaining: multi-tenancy (deferred). Takeaways: Cursor CLI effective for multi-file changes, Prisma migrations need shadow DB workaround.

calendarEvents.title: Weekly Monday 9am check-in
calendarEvents.why: Skim memory files and summarize anything needing human follow-up.

activities.message: Completed overnight critical fixes: verified SSE implementation uses fs.readFile (no localhost fetch), confirmed absolute paths in route.ts.
Audited all 5 agent models. Updated Claw Prime, Forge, and Relay to confirmed free OpenRouter models.
Completed full mock audit: 18 findings across all screens. Saved to data/mock-audit.json.
Replaced mock memories/docs/calendar with real data from workspace memory/*.md, cron jobs, and .md files.

suggestedTools.title: One-click JSON export for tasks + events
suggestedTools.rationale: You iterate quickly on the board; backups before DB will prevent thrash.

suggestedTools.title: Slack/Telegram mirror for heartbeat summaries
suggestedTools.rationale: You balance Discord + other channels; mirrored digests reduce context switching.

suggestedTools.title: Risk tag on tasks waiting for human review
suggestedTools.rationale: Several tasks gate on policy; a dedicated queue speeds approvals.

suggestedTools.title: Doc diff viewer for PRDs vs implementation
suggestedTools.rationale: Architecture and PRD docs drift; inline diff would catch scope creep early.

suggestedTools.title: Calendar import from ICS (read-only)
suggestedTools.rationale: Human blocks often live in external calendars first.

suggestedTools.title: Office focus mode dimming idle agents
suggestedTools.rationale: Reduces visual noise when only one worker is active overnight.

suggestedTools.title: Project momentum sparkline (7-day activity)
suggestedTools.rationale: Neglected projects need a gentle trend, not just a static health label.

suggestedTools.title: Memory graph: link traversal
suggestedTools.rationale: Jumping task to doc to memory visually would match how you reason about decisions.
